# timescale_connector_src_postgres (Resource)

Manages a PostgreSQL source connector for logical replication from an external PostgreSQL database into a Timescale Cloud service.



<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `connection_string` (String, Sensitive) PostgreSQL connection string for the source database (e.g. `postgresql://user:password@host:5432/dbname`).
- `display_name` (String) Human-readable display name for the connector.
- `name` (String) Name for the source configuration.
- `service_id` (String) The Timescale Cloud service ID to replicate data into.

### Optional

- `enabled` (Boolean) Whether the connector is enabled (default: true).
- `ssh_tunnel` (Attributes) Optional SSH tunnel configuration for connecting to the source database through a bastion host. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--ssh_tunnel))
- `table_sync_workers` (Number) Number of parallel workers for table synchronization (default: 4).
- `tables` (Block List) Tables to replicate from the source database. If a table does not exist on the target service, the connector creates it automatically. Table configurations (`table_mapping`, `publication_name`) are immutable once added — changing them causes the provider to automatically drop the table from the connector and re-add it with the new configuration. The actual table in the target database is not dropped. **Warning**: re-adding a table triggers a full re-sync of all the table's data. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--tables))

### Read-Only

- `created_at` (String) Timestamp when the connector was created.
- `id` (String) Unique identifier for the connector.
- `source_id` (String) Unique identifier for the source configuration.
- `status` (String) Current connector status (ok, warning, error, paused).

<a id="nestedatt--ssh_tunnel"></a>
### Nested Schema for `ssh_tunnel`

Required:

- `name` (String) Name for the SSH tunnel configuration.

Optional:

- `host` (String) SSH host (bastion server) for the tunnel connection.
- `port` (Number) SSH port for the tunnel connection.
- `username` (String) SSH username for the tunnel connection.

Read-Only:

- `public_key` (String) Server-generated public key. Add this to the `authorized_keys` file on your SSH bastion host.
- `ssh_tunnel_id` (String) Unique identifier for the SSH tunnel configuration.


<a id="nestedblock--tables"></a>
### Nested Schema for `tables`

Required:

- `schema_name` (String) Schema name of the source table.
- `table_name` (String) Name of the source table.

Optional:

- `hypertable_spec` (Attributes) Optional hypertable configuration for tables that the connector creates on the target. When provided and the target table does not already exist, the connector creates it as a hypertable with the specified dimensions. If the target table already exists, this setting is a no-op. **Create-only**: this can only be set when adding a new table to the connector and cannot be changed afterward. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--tables--hypertable_spec))
- `publication_name` (String) Existing PostgreSQL publication name to use for this table. If not provided the connector will create one.
- `table_mapping` (Attributes) Optional mapping to a different table name on the target service. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--tables--table_mapping))

<a id="nestedatt--tables--hypertable_spec"></a>
### Nested Schema for `tables.hypertable_spec`

Required:

- `primary_dimension` (Attributes) Primary time dimension for the hypertable. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--tables--hypertable_spec--primary_dimension))

Optional:

- `secondary_dimensions` (Attributes List) Optional secondary dimensions for the hypertable. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--tables--hypertable_spec--secondary_dimensions))

<a id="nestedatt--tables--hypertable_spec--primary_dimension"></a>
### Nested Schema for `tables.hypertable_spec.primary_dimension`

Required:

- `column_name` (String) Name of the time column (e.g. `created_at`).
- `partition_interval` (String) Chunk time interval (e.g. `7d`, `1h`, `168h`).


<a id="nestedatt--tables--hypertable_spec--secondary_dimensions"></a>
### Nested Schema for `tables.hypertable_spec.secondary_dimensions`

Optional:

- `hash` (Attributes) Hash (space) dimension. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--tables--hypertable_spec--secondary_dimensions--hash))
- `range` (Attributes) Range (time-based) dimension. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--tables--hypertable_spec--secondary_dimensions--range))

<a id="nestedatt--tables--hypertable_spec--secondary_dimensions--hash"></a>
### Nested Schema for `tables.hypertable_spec.secondary_dimensions.hash`

Required:

- `column_name` (String) Column name for the hash dimension.
- `number_partitions` (Number) Number of hash partitions.


<a id="nestedatt--tables--hypertable_spec--secondary_dimensions--range"></a>
### Nested Schema for `tables.hypertable_spec.secondary_dimensions.range`

Required:

- `column_name` (String) Column name for the range dimension.
- `partition_interval` (String) Partition interval for the range dimension.




<a id="nestedatt--tables--table_mapping"></a>
### Nested Schema for `tables.table_mapping`

Required:

- `schema_name` (String) Target schema name.
- `table_name` (String) Target table name.
